summary: 2026-06-25 14:27:41 PDT - Reviewed the branch against reviews/design-review-loop.md. I found two spec mismatches: one workflow contradiction that can allow an unreviewed redesign to merge, and one README omission against AC13.

findings.title: Redesign path still offers merge
findings.claim: AC11 requires an accepted approach/redesign fix to route to re-review, not straight to merge, but step 4 still says to present exactly the choice "re-review ... or merge" every time. The added line 23 says not to offer a straight merge for redesigns, so the procedure is internally contradictory and can still lead Claude to offer the forbidden merge option after a redesign.
findings.suggestion: Make step 4 conditional: if the latest approved fixes include an approach/redesign fix, stop with re-review as the only route; otherwise present the normal re-review-or-merge fork.

findings.title: README omits review override args
findings.claim: AC13 requires README.md to describe the `/review approach` and `/review correctness` override args, but the new `/review` row only describes the default approach-pass-gates-correctness flow and README.md contains no mention of either override.
findings.suggestion: Add a short note in the `/review` row or nearby text that `/review approach` forces the approach pass and `/review correctness` skips straight to the correctness pass.
